name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) brings together PhD students and young researchers to explore all aspects of AI. It offers a five‑day intensive program of introductory and advanced courses, specialized tutorials, and a rich social programme, including a reception at Vienna City Hall. Held in Vienna, Austria, the school provides networking opportunities with over 30 international lecturers across multiple parallel tracks.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
venue: Skeikampen conference hotel, near Oslo, Norway
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC countries and doctoral candidates, and focuses on multilingual large language models, open‑science practices, and European language resources. The program combines technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and field trips, and is fully subsidised by the OpenEuroLLM project with free registration and organized bus transfers from Oslo Airport.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The ProbAI School 2026, a Probabilistic AI School, will be held in Vilnius, Lithuania. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment featuring state-of-the-art expertise in machine learning and artificial intelligence. The programme welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals for hands‑on learning and community building.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
venue: Durham University, Durham, England, UK
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England, UK. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a wide range of topics in computer vision, delivered by researchers from leading UK and international groups. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: University of Oxford, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 is a specialised AI school hosted by the University of Oxford with an online component. It offers three customizable tracks taught by world‑renowned experts from leading academic and industry AI labs. Participants can mix and match tracks to align with their learning goals and stay at the forefront of AI theory and applications.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions and student presentations. The program is held at the Happy Tammsvik resort and includes indoor and outdoor activities, with all accommodation and meals sponsored for participants.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
venue: Università di Genova, Genova, Italy
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School, a Machine Learning Crash Course, takes place at the University of Genoa in Genoa, Italy. It offers an introductory, hands‑on program on fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms for undergraduate and graduate students as well as professionals, with lectures and lab sessions covering topics such as statistical learning, optimization, kernel methods and neural networks. The course is held in person, provides university credit, includes a final test with notebook work and a report, and is free for UniGe and IIT affiliates while other participants pay a modest fee.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day event held at the National Robotarium, Heriot‑Watt University in Edinburgh, Scotland. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI for robotics, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art technology. The programme targets master’s‑level students and industry practitioners, providing networking opportunities with academics and industry leaders.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
venue: Hotel Dei Pini, Alghero, Italy
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at about 70 pre‑selected PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and opportunities for scholarships and paper presentations.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
venue: Porto, Maia, Portugal
description: The AIces 2026 International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence takes place in Porto and Maia, Portugal from 30 March to 2 April 2026. It targets graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering multidisciplinary lectures, parallel courses, and a symposium on cognition, ethics, and society. Participants can attend in person or online, with recordings available for 45 days after the event and a certificate of participation.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
venue: Umeå University, Umeå, Sweden
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems brings together researchers and practitioners to discuss interdisciplinary challenges. It offers lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session and a social program to foster networking and professional exchange. The event is held at Umeå University in Sweden from 11 to 13 March 2026 and is open to students, faculty and other participants, with a registration fee covering logistics and meals.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
venue: Escandille, Autrans, France
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) 2026 takes place at the Escandille in Autrans, France. It aims to bring together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human‑robot interaction to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through courses, talks, and hands‑on sessions. The program features expert speakers covering topics like affective signal processing, conversational speech synthesis, and multimodal dialogue, and offers informal networking in the scenic Vercors mountains.

name: Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) Melbourne
venue: Sandridge Events Centre, Port Melbourne, Melbourne, Australia
description: The 2026 Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) takes place in Melbourne, Australia at the Sandridge Events Centre. It targets 50 hand‑picked PhD students and early‑career researchers, offering two weeks of lectures and tutorials covering core theory to post‑LLM research topics such as deep learning, generative modeling and geometric deep learning. World‑class speakers from academia and industry present, and stipends are available for selected participants.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held at UiT The Arctic University of Norway in Tromsø. It focuses on deep learning techniques, covering topics such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time series prediction, and speech recognition. The school targets researchers and practitioners who wish to learn state-of-the-art methods and foster collaborations within the deep learning community.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
venue: Swostishree Gurukul, Sanobharyang, Kathmandu, Nepal
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, labs, and project work in Nepal. It covers topics from fundamental mathematics and statistics to cutting‑edge AI methods and applications across domains such as computer vision, NLP, and medical imaging. The school targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty, and industry professionals, fostering collaboration among over 500 in‑person attendees and thousands of online participants.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School brings together students and researchers for intensive training in modern machine‑learning methods. It features lectures and tutorials delivered by leading experts from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, and the University of Oxford. The event takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro, providing participants with both academic networking opportunities and a scenic summer setting.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
venue: University of Split Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering and Naval Architecture, Split, Croatia
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is hosted by the University of Split Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering and Naval Architecture in Split, Croatia. It offers a five‑day program of keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions covering topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, reinforcement learning, and ethics in machine learning. The school targets doctoral and exceptional master students, academics, and practitioners, emphasizing diversity, networking, and the opportunity to present research in poster sessions.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program jointly organized by MoroccoAI, INPT, IMT Nord Europe, and Soft Centre. It targets master and Ph.D. students, post‑docs, young researchers, and professionals, offering theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, project work, and networking opportunities with leading AI experts. The school aims to equip participants with cutting‑edge AI knowledge and practical skills while fostering the next generation of AI leaders in Morocco.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
venue: NCSR Demokritos, Athens, Greece
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 will be held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. The program, organized by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, and Heriot‑Watt University, focuses on advanced topics in natural language processing. It runs from 2 to 8 September 2026 and is linked to the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence taking place shortly after.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It offers theoretical instruction and hands‑on experience with clustering, GANs, self‑supervised and energy‑based models, emphasizing real‑world applications across domains. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML knowledge who are considering graduate study or research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
venue: Caen, Normandy, France
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI is organized in Caen, Normandy, France, targeting PhD students, post‑doctoral researchers and scientists. It covers machine learning for scientific data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops and case studies. The program emphasizes deep learning skills, requiring participants to have experience with Python, NumPy, and PyTorch, and aims to accelerate scientific progress through AI.

name: LUT Summer School
venue: LUT University, Lappeenranta, Finland
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive programme offering courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and inventive thinking that combine business and technology perspectives. The courses, worth 3 ECTS each, are open to bachelor’s and master’s students from any university and include workshops, group work and a lively social programme set in the Finnish summer environment. Participants study on the Lappeenranta campus from 3 to 7 August 2026 and benefit from access to university facilities, temporary LUT accounts and networking opportunities with peers and experts.

name: Armenian LLM Summer School 2025
description: The LLM Summer School 2026 is an intensive program for students and researchers focusing on the latest advancements in Large Language Models. It will be held in Yerevan, Armenia and will feature a combination of lectures, hands‑on workshops, and a hackathon where participants can apply their knowledge to innovative projects. The event is part of an annual series that aims to provide comprehensive training and networking opportunities in the field of LLMs.

name: Armenian LLM Summer School 2025
venue: AI9 Startup Campus, Yerevan, Armenia
description: The Armenia LLM Summer School 2026 is an intensive week of lectures and hands‑on sessions on Large Language Models held at the AI9 Startup Campus in Yerevan, Armenia. It combines in‑person and online tracks, includes a 24‑hour hackathon on August 8, and offers practical workshops, GPU access, and networking opportunities for students, researchers, and engineers. Participants can benefit from financial aid, fee waivers, and a certificate of participation upon completion.
